mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates lower the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate crossing above 5.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during monetary tightening cycles reflects resilient supply-side capacity that absorbs rate hikes, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production changes during tightening periods yield negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of observations, or predictive accuracy drops below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate income and corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or signal direction reverses in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index exceeding 260.0 triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 260.0, or threshold model fails to reject null hypothesis of no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing quarterly GDP growth and rising retail sales indicates unsustainable consumption debt, leading to a demand collapse and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between declining GDP and rising retail sales fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance, or out-of-sample R-squared remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during recovery phases signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks that constrain output growth, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production changes during recovery periods yield positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or predictive accuracy falls below chance.
